rationale: The citation to Yang and Allenby (2003) is not a passing mention; it is used to justify why estimating social intercorrelation is important for consumer choice research. In the model section, the authors explicitly position their specification as following prior spatial-model work that includes Yang and Allenby (2003), indicating methodological grounding. Although the 2013 paper does not directly re-estimate Yang and Allenby's empirical model, it builds on that established framework to study sampling- and topology-driven bias in the social intercorrelation parameter.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Yang and Allenby (2003) as part of the methodological and conceptual base for modeling interdependent consumer preferences via spatial intercorrelation. It then develops new analysis on how sampling and network topology affect recovery of that intercorrelation parameter.
